robot_notes: Unitree H1 (19-dof body) + 2x Shadow Hand (21-dof each). action_space is Box(-1, 1, (61,), float32) for ALL 10 tasks. observation_space is Box(-inf, inf, (D,), float64) where D varies per task because manipulation tasks append object state.
success_definition: OFFICIAL HumanoidBench protocol: episode_success = (episode_total_reward >= success_bar). Only h1hand-push-v0 and h1hand-package-v0 also expose info['success'] in env.step()'s info dict; for the other 8 tasks, predict.py MUST sum rewards over the episode and threshold against success_bar from this file. Do NOT rely on info.get('success', 0) for non-push/package tasks — it will always be 0 and your success_rate will silently be 0.
evaluator_protocol: predict.py reports per-episode 'reward' (== episode_total_reward); the evaluator recomputes success = (reward >= success_bar) for every task using the bars in this file. predict.py MAY also report a per-episode 'success' field; if it disagrees with the recomputation, the evaluator-side recomputation wins.
deterministic_eval_notes: seed `k` means env.reset(seed=k). Use deterministic policy actions (e.g. SB3 model.predict(obs, deterministic=True)) — sampling adds 10-20% variance on the same seed and breaks reproducibility.
